title: The Wall Holding Back the End of the World
_note: Narration text is written TTS-friendly (phonetic spellings for names); on-screen captions use the proper spelling. Verify name pronunciations with a short test render before generating the full narration (keeps ElevenLabs usage low).

segments.text: Somewhere on this earth is a wall you won't find on any map. And behind it, the Quran says, is a force that will one day pour out and signal the end of the world.
segments.caption: There is a wall you won't find on any map.

segments.text: This isn't folklore. It's in the Quran. The story of a mysterious traveler called Dhul-Qarnayn, a king given power and a path across the entire earth.
segments.caption: Surah Al-Kahf · 18:83
segments.say: This isn't folklore. It's in the Quran. The story of a mysterious traveler called Dhool-Qar-nayn, a king given power and a path across the entire earth.

segments.text: He traveled west, until he reached a place where the sun seemed to set into dark, murky water. Then he turned east, to a people living exposed under the sun. And then, a valley between two mountains, and a people who could barely understand a word.
They were terrified, and they begged him: Gog and Magog spread corruption in this land. Build a barrier between us and them.
The wall is temporary. And the Prophet, peace be upon him, told us: every day, Gog and Magog dig at it until they almost break through, and every day it is restored. Until the day it isn't.
segments.caption: Tirmidhi 3153 (hasan)

segments.text: When they finally pour out, it is one of the great signs that the end has begun.
segments.caption: Quran 21:96 · Sahih Muslim

segments.text: So somewhere out there, the digging continues. And the Quran told us, fourteen hundred years ago.
segments.caption: Follow for the stories the Quran actually tells.

sources: Quran 21:96-97; Sahih Muslim - Gog & Magog as a sign of the end
